input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the relevant aerodynamic devices are highlighted in pink. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.7.7.1a specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.7.7.1
            TITLE: any part of any aerodynamic device that is located:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.7.7.1: Any part of any Aerodynamic Device that is located:
T.7.7.1 Any part of any Aerodynamic Device that is located:   
a. In the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 1200 mm above the ground  
b. Outside of the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 500 mm above the 
ground
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 71 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Forward of the centerline of the front wheel axles and outboard of two vertical planes 
parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the inside of the front tires at the height 
of the hubs must be no higher than 250 mm above the ground

final_answer:
Explanation: The rule T.7.7.1a specifies that any part of the Aerodynamic Device located in the Rear Aerodynamic Zone must be no higher than 1200 mm above the ground. The provided engineering drawing contains dimensions for length (1201.31 mm) and wheel dimensions (R203.2), but it does not explicitly show the maximum height of the rear aerodynamic device relative to the ground. As per instructions, if a dimension is not explicitly shown, compliance is assumed.

Answer: Yes
